_comment: Public vs private hospital median wait times for elective procedures — Australia
_note: Public wait times are AIHW national medians for FY 2023-24. Private wait times are industry estimates — actual wait depends on specialist availability. 90th percentile means 90% of patients waited less than this time.
source: Australian Institute of Health and Welfare (AIHW) — Elective Surgery Waiting Times 2023-24

procedures.label: Knee Replacement

publicWaitDays.description: National median 265 days; 1 in 10 patients waits over 614 days

privateWaitDays.description: Typically 4–12 weeks from referral to surgery with private cover

procedures.context: Knee replacements are among the longest public waits due to high demand. Pain and mobility impact can be severe during the wait.
procedures.coverRequired: Silver or Gold (joint replacements are excluded from Bronze and below)

procedures.label: Hip Replacement

publicWaitDays.description: National median 175 days; 1 in 10 patients waits over 432 days

privateWaitDays.description: Typically 4–10 weeks from referral to surgery with private cover

procedures.context: Hip replacements are highly effective at eliminating chronic hip pain and restoring mobility. Delay reduces quality of life significantly.
procedures.coverRequired: Silver or Gold (joint replacements excluded from Bronze and below)

procedures.label: Cataract Surgery

publicWaitDays.description: National median 88 days; 1 in 10 patients waits over 257 days

privateWaitDays.description: Typically 2–6 weeks from referral to surgery with private cover

procedures.context: Cataracts progressively cloud vision. Surgery is straightforward and quick. Waiting significantly impacts quality of life and driving safety.
procedures.coverRequired: Bronze or above (cataracts are included from Bronze)

procedures.label: Septoplasty (Deviated Septum)

publicWaitDays.description: National median 332 days — one of the longest public waits. 1 in 10 waits nearly 2 years.

privateWaitDays.description: Typically 4–10 weeks from referral to surgery with private cover

procedures.context: Septoplasty corrects a deviated nasal septum causing breathing difficulties. Public wait is extremely long despite the relatively simple procedure.

procedures.label: Tonsillectomy

publicWaitDays.description: National median 179 days for adults; children may vary

privateWaitDays.description: Typically 2–6 weeks from referral to surgery with private cover

procedures.context: Tonsillectomy is often needed for recurrent tonsillitis or obstructive sleep apnoea. Frequent illness during a long public wait is common.

procedures.label: Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)

publicWaitDays.description: National median 18 days — cardiac surgery is prioritised in the public system

procedures.context: Cardiac surgery is Category 1 (urgent) and treated promptly in public hospitals. The private advantage here is primarily about choice of surgeon and hospital, not wait time.
procedures.coverRequired: Gold (cardiac surgery is restricted in Silver and excluded in Bronze/Basic)

procedures.label: Shoulder Surgery (Rotator Cuff)

publicWaitDays.description: National median approximately 245 days for shoulder reconstructions

privateWaitDays.description: Typically 3–8 weeks from referral to surgery with private cover

procedures.context: Shoulder rotator cuff repairs and reconstructions have very long public waits. Pain and reduced arm function impact daily life and work.
procedures.coverRequired: Silver or Gold (joint investigations may be covered from Bronze)

procedures.label: Colonoscopy

publicWaitDays.description: National median approximately 122 days for elective colonoscopy

procedures.context: Colonoscopy is used for cancer screening, polyp removal, and bowel disease diagnosis. Early access can be critical for cancer detection.

keyInsights.coverTierMatters: Joint replacements (hip/knee/shoulder) require Silver or Gold cover — not available on Basic or Bronze. Planning surgery? Check your tier before needing it.
keyInsights.waitingPeriodRule: A 12-month waiting period applies to most hospital procedures when you first join or upgrade cover. Plan ahead.
